items.q: Why did William of Normandy claim the English throne in 1066?
items.correct: He claimed Edward the Confessor had promised him the throne in 1051 and that Harold Godwinson had sworn an oath to support that claim in 1064.
items.misconception: William invaded because he was the strongest king in Europe and wanted more land.
items.random_wrong: William was Edward the Confessor's eldest son.
items.why: Students treat the invasion as raw conquest, missing that William framed it as enforcing a legitimate hereditary and oath-based claim — legitimacy mattered hugely in 11th-century politics.

items.q: Why was Vesalius significant in the history of medicine?
items.correct: His 1543 De Humani Corporis Fabrica corrected Galen's errors via human dissection and accurate illustration, founding modern anatomy though not curing disease.
items.misconception: Vesalius discovered the cause of disease and ended humoral theory.
items.why: Vesalius improved anatomy but disease theory still ran on humours for centuries. Students conflate progress in one area (structure) with progress in another (cause of illness).

items.q: How was the Cuban Missile Crisis resolved?
items.correct: A secret deal: the USSR withdrew missiles from Cuba; the US publicly pledged not to invade Cuba and secretly agreed to remove Jupiter missiles from Turkey.
items.misconception: Kennedy stared Khrushchev down and the Soviets backed off unconditionally.
items.random_wrong: The crisis ended when Castro persuaded Khrushchev to withdraw.
items.why: The Turkey concession was kept secret for decades, fuelling the myth of unilateral US victory. The actual resolution was a negotiated compromise — important for understanding nuclear diplomacy.

items.q: Who led the Bolsheviks in October 1917?
items.why: Correct answer: Lenin. “Stalin” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
It's NEP. “Five-Year Plan” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year did the October Revolution occur?
items.why: The real figure is 1917 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
